problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Glial cell activation/suppression in target brain region
hypothesis: The key to Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? is Glial cell activation/suppression in target brain region — specifically Glial cell activation/suppression — which would help 12,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Longitudinal patient cohort with 26 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
